steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined claim states that Beloved resembles magical realist novels in its juxtaposition of literary realism with Black American cultural traditions. A supporting quotation must show both elements — realism and those cultural traditions — working together in that relationship.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Much of the interest of Beloved derives from the productive tension between its competing influences—namely, Black American cultural traditions and the magical realism of Latin America.": frames the two influences as competitors and introduces Latin American magical realism as a separate influence, which doesn't match the claim about juxtaposing realism with Black American traditions. ✗
  • Choice B — "The cultural traditions of the Black American community, which figure so prominently in the magical realist tradition of Latin America, permit realistic as well as antirealistic scenarios—much as Beloved does.": wrongly attributes Black American traditions to Latin American magical realism — a factual distortion that contradicts the passage's point. ✗
  • Choice C — "Even though Beloved alternates between realistic and antirealistic modes of representation, the influence of Black American cultural traditions remains constant throughout the novel.": says the two elements alternate rather than being juxtaposed, and separates the cultural traditions from the mode-switching. ✗
  • Choice D — "Although much of Beloved conforms to the conventions of realistic fiction, Toni Morrison also incorporates elements drawn from Black American cultural traditions that transcend and expose the limitations of realism.": directly shows the realistic mode alongside Black American traditions operating against it — exactly the juxtaposition the claim describes. ✓
That is the correct answer.
